description: In the early 1960s, the public perception of the drug thalidomide shifted from a 'wonder drug' for morning sickness to a symbol of catastrophic regulatory failure. The revelation of thousands of birth defects led to a massive public outcry and a permanent overhaul of drug testing requirements.

key_events.description: Dr. William McBride publishes letter in The Lancet linking drug to birth defects.
Distillers Company begins withdrawal of thalidomide from the UK market.
UK government establishes the Committee on the Safety of Drugs (the Dunlop Committee).

calibration_notes: Provides a high-intensity shift scenario where an established public benefit is rapidly eclipsed by a catastrophic safety revelation.
